data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix uses a microservices architecture which allows for independent deployment and scaling of services.
data.technical_spec: Services communicate via API gateways, ensuring loose coupling and high cohesion.

data.title: Backend Resilience Patterns
data.description: Incorporates patterns like Hystrix for circuit breaking to prevent cascading failures.
data.technical_spec: Uses bulkheads, timeouts, and fallbacks to isolate failures and protect dependencies.

data.title: Global Load Balancing
data.description: Global load balancing is implemented to distribute traffic effectively across regions.
data.technical_spec: Uses geographically distributed DNS and edge servers for traffic management.

data.title: Scalable Data Architecture
data.description: Adopts NoSQL databases and data lakes to manage and analyze large-scale data.
data.technical_spec: Employs cloud-native solutions like Cassandra, AWS S3 for storage needs.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Netflix's CDN, Open Connect, improves video delivery speed and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Strategically placed edge servers cache content close to viewers.
